## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Was mechanical ceremony repair; now carries a net-new Rust static-analysis fixture with a real design surface. Three mechanical tracks (ceremony retirement cleanup, claimed-done fixes, verification sweep) frame one design-laden track: a BCG command-compliance linter in theurge. The linter needs judgment; the rest is mechanical. The locked decisions below are settled — do not relitigate them; mount-time minting of the new module/fixture name is the open naming call.
  
  ## Locked decisions (2026-05-31 design conversation, Opus 4.8)
  
  - **Track 2 is a Rust theurge lint, not bash.** The external-command audit becomes a static-analysis fixture in theurge's **fast test suite** — not a bash step in `rbq_qualify_release()`. It rides `rbw-ts.fast` (dev loop) and, via the `complete` suite, `rbw-tr` (release gate), which the ceremony's Step 4 runs pre-strip. That coverage is what lets ceremony **Step 2 be deleted**.
  - **BCG is the single source of truth.** Encode the allowlists from BCG section "Command Dependency Discipline": the POSIX floor, the declared-dependency set (RBS0 inventory + `openssl`), and the **eviction table — enforced**, failing with BCG's prescribed replacement (e.g. `grep` -> `case`/`[[ =~ ]]`). This is stricter than today's Step 2, whose hand-rolled POSIX list wrongly permits evicted commands.
  - **Algorithm: two-pass, function-aware.** Collect every locally-defined function across the corpus; then flag each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, POSIX allowlist, declared deps}, and fail-with-replacement on the eviction table. Soundness rests on presumed BCG-compliance (shellcheck already gates the corpus in `rbq_qualify_fast`) — a command-position scan suffices; no heredoc/case parser.
  - **Checks everything pre-veiling.** Lints ALL `Tools/` bash including `vov_veiled/`. The linter carries **no veil-scope knowledge** — it is a linter, not veiling logic, and must not couple to either packaging path (`vof` kit-parcel collection, or the ceremony strip). The only partition is by **execution environment**: kit-bash (strict BCG) vs GCB-bash (`rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/` — looser; gcloud et al. present). **Encode the GCB-domain allowlist now**; do not skip those dirs.
  - **First-run cleanup is in-pace.** Checking veiled bash for the first time may surface real eviction-table violations; fix them in this pace, do not split to a follow-on. Expect the first run not to land green.
  - **Track 4 resolved.** `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM` pointing at `recipebottle-staging.git` is **intended**. No remote mutation. Ensure no ceremony/doc text asserts the non-staging URL as canonical.
  
  ## Tracks
  
  ### Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup
  
  In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired `cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase `readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) — replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep `git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add (`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2 (Track 2).
  
  ### Track 2 — BCG command-lint fixture (locked above)
  
  Build per the locked decisions. Model on the host-side fast-fixture pattern (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`: `dir: &Path -> rbtdre_Verdict`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, added to the `fast` suite). Mint a fresh anchor word — `audit` is taken (image-domain cult verb). Then delete ceremony Step 2.
  
  ### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done
  
  Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` -> `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C (Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-completeness check present) verify-and-close.
  
  ### Track 5 — Verification sweep
  
  After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.
  
  ## Verification gates
  
  Each track presented before executing. Review point before committing the ceremony diff. The lint fixture's first full run (including veiled bash) is a discovery step, not a pass gate — surface violations before fixing. No remote mutation. No auto-wrap.
  
  ## References
  
  - `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — ceremony repair target
  - `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` section "Command Dependency Discipline" — allowlist source of truth (POSIX floor, eviction table, declared-dep principle)
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`, `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fast-fixture shape + suite/manifest registration
  - `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — `rbq_qualify_fast`/`rbq_qualify_release`; shellcheck sibling; Step 2 deletion rationale
  - `Tools/vok/vof/` — existing kit-parcel veiling (`vofr_collect` excludes vov_veiled); the linter must NOT couple to it
  - Commits b7c1dbdd (retirement), 5dc8dcc6 (README restructure), b63dc6cf (foundry colophon rbw-h* -> rbw-f*)
  - Absorbed ₢A8AAA D4+D5; D3 (CLAUDE.consumer glossary) landed on 5dc8dcc6, excluded

a35c5a493  ₢BBABM    Fix two cupel lexer false-positive classes and exclude ABANDONED dead code. (1) Array literals NAME=( ... ) were treated as subshell command groups, recording bare flag elements (-o, -U) as commands; now detected after an assignment word and skipped as data via a new balanced-paren helper. (2) Nested parens inside $(( ... )) / (( ... )) arithmetic terminated the skip at the first )), leaking the remainder (0xFFFFFFFF) as commands; the helper now balances paren depth and skips quoted segments. (3) Per operator decision, the corpus walk now skips any ABANDONED* directory — dead code is not held to the live command-dependency discipline. Four new unit tests; all 134 theurge tests pass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
24337f79a  ₢BBABM    Add case…esac state tracking to the cupel lexer. The first discovery run produced ~400 false positives because case-branch patterns (glob *, HTTP status codes, mode/enum values, getopts flags) sit at command position and were mis-recorded as commands. A three-state per-case frame stack (subject/pattern/body) now suppresses pattern tokens, treats `|` as alternation inside patterns rather than a pipe, and scans only branch-body command lists — handling nested case and commands following esac. Six unit tests pin the behavior. All 131 theurge tests pass; builds clean under deny(warnings).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
5d59fdb08  ₢BBABM    Add cupel BCG command-dependency lint fixture to theurge. New rbtdru_cupel.rs implements a two-pass function-aware command-position lexer over all Tools/ bash: pass 1 unions every locally-defined function across the corpus, pass 2 flags each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, BCG POSIX floor, RBS0 declared deps}, failing-with-replacement on BCG's enforced eviction table. Partitions kit-bash (strict) from GCB-bash (any Tools/rbk/rbgj* path — looser, evictions tolerated, gcloud et al. allowed) by path prefix rather than a hardcoded dir list, covering all seven rbgj* job groups. Two Independent cases (kit, gcb). Registered in manifest (empty required-colophons), lib.rs, the fixtures registry, and the six suites carrying the fast fixtures (fast/service/crucible/complete/gauntlet/skirmish) — riding rbw-ts.fast for the dev loop and the complete suite for the rbw-tr release gate. rbtdtu_cupel.rs adds 27 unit tests pinning the lexer (heredoc/here-string/arithmetic/case/quote/comment handling), assignment + keyword classification, function collection, and the kit-vs-gcb classify polarity. Builds clean under deny(warnings); all 126 theurge tests pass. Anchor word cupel (assay vessel) sits in theurge's metallurgy family beside crucible/calibrant.    
40c429ddb  ₢BBAA8    Wrap docker login in bounded transient-retry, closing the asymmetry with the curl/poll boundary that already tolerates the same moby#44350 premature-timeout class. docker login is the one login/pull/push verb with no internal retry and a hardcoded non-configurable 15s daemon->registry auth timeout; against a healthy-but-slow GAR backend it fails where the endpoint is fine (the BBAAX gauntlet failure mode). New stateless rbgo_docker_login retries only the surveyed Go-stdlib signature and fails fast on real auth errors, preserving clean-failure semantics; reuses the RBGC HTTP retry budget. Applied at all six foundry login sites (rbfl/rbfr/rbob/rbfd); the depot-regime tripwire (rbndb), a separate dependency cluster that does not reach rbgo, gains the same retry in its own helper sharing only the new RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE constant out of rbgc. pull/push left untouched — docker retries them internally.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Was mechanical ceremony repair; now carries a net-new Rust static-analysis fixture with a real design surface. Three mechanical tracks (ceremony retirement cleanup, claimed-done fixes, verification sweep) frame one design-laden track: a BCG command-compliance linter in theurge. The linter needs judgment; the rest is mechanical. The locked decisions below are settled — do not relitigate them; mount-time minting of the new module/fixture name is the open naming call.
  
  ## Locked decisions (2026-05-31 design conversation, Opus 4.8)
  
  - **Track 2 is a Rust theurge lint, not bash.** The external-command audit becomes a static-analysis fixture in theurge's **fast test suite** — not a bash step in `rbq_qualify_release()`. It rides `rbw-ts.fast` (dev loop) and, via the `complete` suite, `rbw-tr` (release gate), which the ceremony's Step 4 runs pre-strip. That coverage is what lets ceremony **Step 2 be deleted**.
  - **BCG is the single source of truth.** Encode the allowlists from BCG section "Command Dependency Discipline": the POSIX floor, the declared-dependency set (RBS0 inventory + `openssl`), and the **eviction table — enforced**, failing with BCG's prescribed replacement (e.g. `grep` -> `case`/`[[ =~ ]]`). This is stricter than today's Step 2, whose hand-rolled POSIX list wrongly permits evicted commands.
  - **Algorithm: two-pass, function-aware.** Collect every locally-defined function across the corpus; then flag each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, POSIX allowlist, declared deps}, and fail-with-replacement on the eviction table. Soundness rests on presumed BCG-compliance (shellcheck already gates the corpus in `rbq_qualify_fast`) — a command-position scan suffices; no heredoc/case parser.
  - **Checks everything pre-veiling.** Lints ALL `Tools/` bash including `vov_veiled/`. The linter carries **no veil-scope knowledge** — it is a linter, not veiling logic, and must not couple to either packaging path (`vof` kit-parcel collection, or the ceremony strip). The only partition is by **execution environment**: kit-bash (strict BCG) vs GCB-bash (`rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/` — looser; gcloud et al. present). **Encode the GCB-domain allowlist now**; do not skip those dirs.
  - **First-run cleanup is in-pace.** Checking veiled bash for the first time may surface real eviction-table violations; fix them in this pace, do not split to a follow-on. Expect the first run not to land green.
  - **Track 4 resolved.** `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM` pointing at `recipebottle-staging.git` is **intended**. No remote mutation. Ensure no ceremony/doc text asserts the non-staging URL as canonical.
  
  ## Tracks
  
  ### Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup
  
  In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired `cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase `readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) — replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep `git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add (`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2 (Track 2).
  
  ### Track 2 — BCG command-lint fixture (locked above)
  
  Build per the locked decisions. Model on the host-side fast-fixture pattern (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`: `dir: &Path -> rbtdre_Verdict`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, added to the `fast` suite). Mint a fresh anchor word — `audit` is taken (image-domain cult verb). Then delete ceremony Step 2.
  
  ### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done
  
  Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` -> `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C (Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-completeness check present) verify-and-close.
  
  ### Track 5 — Verification sweep
  
  After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.
  
  ## Verification gates
  
  Each track presented before executing. Review point before committing the ceremony diff. The lint fixture's first full run (including veiled bash) is a discovery step, not a pass gate — surface violations before fixing. No remote mutation. No auto-wrap.
  
  ## References
  
  - `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — ceremony repair target

  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`, `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fast-fixture shape + suite/manifest registration
  - `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — `rbq_qualify_fast`/`rbq_qualify_release`; shellcheck sibling; Step 2 deletion rationale
  - `Tools/vok/vof/` — existing kit-parcel veiling (`vofr_collect` excludes vov_veiled); the linter must NOT couple to it

a35c5a493  ₢BBABM    Fix two cupel lexer false-positive classes and exclude ABANDONED dead code. (1) Array literals NAME=( ... ) were treated as subshell command groups, recording bare flag elements (-o, -U) as commands; now detected after an assignment word and skipped as data via a new balanced-paren helper. (2) Nested parens inside $(( ... )) / (( ... )) arithmetic terminated the skip at the first )), leaking the remainder (0xFFFFFFFF) as commands; the helper now balances paren depth and skips quoted segments. (3) Per operator decision, the corpus walk now skips any ABANDONED* directory — dead code is not held to the live command-dependency discipline. Four new unit tests; all 134 theurge tests pass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
24337f79a  ₢BBABM    Add case…esac state tracking to the cupel lexer. The first discovery run produced ~400 false positives because case-branch patterns (glob *, HTTP status codes, mode/enum values, getopts flags) sit at command position and were mis-recorded as commands. A three-state per-case frame stack (subject/pattern/body) now suppresses pattern tokens, treats `|` as alternation inside patterns rather than a pipe, and scans only branch-body command lists — handling nested case and commands following esac. Six unit tests pin the behavior. All 131 theurge tests pass; builds clean under deny(warnings).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
5d59fdb08  ₢BBABM    Add cupel BCG command-dependency lint fixture to theurge. New rbtdru_cupel.rs implements a two-pass function-aware command-position lexer over all Tools/ bash: pass 1 unions every locally-defined function across the corpus, pass 2 flags each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, BCG POSIX floor, RBS0 declared deps}, failing-with-replacement on BCG's enforced eviction table. Partitions kit-bash (strict) from GCB-bash (any Tools/rbk/rbgj* path — looser, evictions tolerated, gcloud et al. allowed) by path prefix rather than a hardcoded dir list, covering all seven rbgj* job groups. Two Independent cases (kit, gcb). Registered in manifest (empty required-colophons), lib.rs, the fixtures registry, and the six suites carrying the fast fixtures (fast/service/crucible/complete/gauntlet/skirmish) — riding rbw-ts.fast for the dev loop and the complete suite for the rbw-tr release gate. rbtdtu_cupel.rs adds 27 unit tests pinning the lexer (heredoc/here-string/arithmetic/case/quote/comment handling), assignment + keyword classification, function collection, and the kit-vs-gcb classify polarity. Builds clean under deny(warnings); all 126 theurge tests pass. Anchor word cupel (assay vessel) sits in theurge's metallurgy family beside crucible/calibrant.    
40c429ddb  ₢BBAA8    Wrap docker login in bounded transient-retry, closing the asymmetry with the curl/poll boundary that already tolerates the same moby#44350 premature-timeout class. docker login is the one login/pull/push verb with no internal retry and a hardcoded non-configurable 15s daemon->registry auth timeout; against a healthy-but-slow GAR backend it fails where the endpoint is fine (the BBAAX gauntlet failure mode). New stateless rbgo_docker_login retries only the surveyed Go-stdlib signature and fails fast on real auth errors, preserving clean-failure semantics; reuses the RBGC HTTP retry budget. Applied at all six foundry login sites (rbfl/rbfr/rbob/rbfd); the depot-regime tripwire (rbndb), a separate dependency cluster that does not reach rbgo, gains the same retry in its own helper sharing only the new RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE constant out of rbgc. pull/push left untouched — docker retries them internally.

content.text: Let me read the paddock from the gazette.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
150	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
152	
153	# jjezs_pace ₢BBABM
154	
155	## Character
156	
157	Was mechanical ceremony repair; now carries a net-new Rust static-analysis fixture with a real design surface. Three mechanical tracks (ceremony retirement cleanup, claimed-done fixes, verification sweep) frame one design-laden track: a BCG command-compliance linter in theurge. The linter needs judgment; the rest is mechanical. The locked decisions below are settled — do not relitigate them; mount-time minting of the new module/fixture name is the open naming call.
158	
159	## Locked decisions (2026-05-31 design conversation, Opus 4.8)
160	
161	- **Track 2 is a Rust theurge lint, not bash.** The external-command audit becomes a static-analysis fixture in theurge's **fast test suite** — not a bash step in `rbq_qualify_release()`. It rides `rbw-ts.fast` (dev loop) and, via the `complete` suite, `rbw-tr` (release gate), which the ceremony's Step 4 runs pre-strip. That coverage is what lets ceremony **Step 2 be deleted**.
162	- **BCG is the single source of truth.** Encode the allowlists from BCG section "Command Dependency Discipline": the POSIX floor, the declared-dependency set (RBS0 inventory + `openssl`), and the **eviction table — enforced**, failing with BCG's prescribed replacement (e.g. `grep` -> `case`/`[[ =~ ]]`). This is stricter than today's Step 2, whose hand-rolled POSIX list wrongly permits evicted commands.
163	- **Algorithm: two-pass, function-aware.** Collect every locally-defined function across the corpus; then flag each command-position token not in {bash builtins, local functions, POSIX allowlist, declared deps}, and fail-with-replacement on the eviction table. Soundness rests on presumed BCG-compliance (shellcheck already gates the corpus in `rbq_qualify_fast`) — a command-position scan suffices; no heredoc/case parser.
164	- **Checks everything pre-veiling.** Lints ALL `Tools/` bash including `vov_veiled/`. The linter carries **no veil-scope knowledge** — it is a linter, not veiling logic, and must not couple to either packaging path (`vof` kit-parcel collection, or the ceremony strip). The only partition is by **execution environment**: kit-bash (strict BCG) vs GCB-bash (`rbgja/`, `rbgjb/`, `rbgjv/` — looser; gcloud et al. present). **Encode the GCB-domain allowlist now**; do not skip those dirs.
165	- **First-run cleanup is in-pace.** Checking veiled bash for the first time may surface real eviction-table violations; fix them in this pace, do not split to a follow-on. Expect the first run not to land green.
166	- **Track 4 resolved.** `OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM` pointing at `recipebottle-staging.git` is **intended**. No remote mutation. Ensure no ceremony/doc text asserts the non-staging URL as canonical.
167	
168	## Tracks
169	
170	### Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup
171	
172	In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired `cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase `readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) — replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep `git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add (`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2 (Track 2).
173	
174	### Track 2 — BCG command-lint fixture (locked above)
175	
176	Build per the locked decisions. Model on the host-side fast-fixture pattern (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`: `dir: &Path -> rbtdre_Verdict`, registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, added to the `fast` suite). Mint a fresh anchor word — `audit` is taken (image-domain cult verb). Then delete ceremony Step 2.
177	
178	### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done
179	
180	Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` -> `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C (Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-completeness check present) verify-and-close.
181	
182	### Track 5 — Verification sweep
183	
184	After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.
185	
186	## Verification gates
187	
188	Each track presented before executing. Review point before committing the ceremony diff. The lint fixture's first full run (including veiled bash) is a discovery step, not a pass gate — surface violations before fixing. No remote mutation. No auto-wrap.
189	
190	## References
191	
192	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — ceremony repair target
193	- `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` section "Command Dependency Discipline" — allowlist source of truth (POSIX floor, eviction table, declared-dep principle)
194	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`, `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fast-fixture shape + suite/manifest registration
195	- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — `rbq_qualify_fast`/`rbq_qualify_release`; shellcheck sibling; Step 2 deletion rationale
196	- `Tools/vok/vof/` — existing kit-parcel veiling (`vofr_collect` excludes vov_veiled); the linter must NOT couple to it
197	- Commits b7c1dbdd (retirement), 5dc8dcc6 (README restructure), b63dc6cf (foundry colophon rbw-h* -> rbw-f*)
198	- Absorbed ₢A8AAA D4+D5; D3 (CLAUDE.consumer glossary) landed on 5dc8dcc6, excluded

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

## Tracks

### Track 1 — Ceremony retirement cleanup

In `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md`: Step 8 — drop the retired `cp README.consumer.md README.md` (keep the `CLAUDE.consumer.md` copy); the lowercase `readme.md` removal is a no-op (case-insensitive alias of the tracked `README.md`) — replace it with defensive `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch index.html .nojekyll`; Step 10 survival list — remove `.nojekyll` and `index.html` (both retired); staging — keep `git add CLAUDE.md` (Step 8 overwrites it), drop the explicit `README.md` add (`git add -u` covers it). Step 2's dependency-source line vanishes with Step 2 (Track 2).

### Track 3 — Verify claimed-done

Item B is a FIX: correct the phantom `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` -> `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh` in ceremony Step 11 (`rbw-tf` is FixtureRun). Items C (Step 9 marshal-zero ordering before the Step 10 strip) and D (Step 3 regime-completeness check present) verify-and-close.

### Track 5 — Verification sweep

After Tracks 1-4 land: retirement grep (no live `index.html`/`README.consumer.md`/`.nojekyll` outside retired gallops/memos); foundry colophon grep (no stale `rbw-h*` foundry refs in consumer-visible sources post b63dc6cf); fast qualify `tt/rbw-tq.QualifyFast.sh`; onboarding smoke (`tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh` + one role track); notch. No auto-wrap — operator decides the close.

## Verification gates

Each track presented before executing. Review point before committing the ceremony diff. The lint fixture's first full run (including veiled bash) is a discovery step, not a pass gate — surface violations before fixing. No remote mutation. No auto-wrap.

## References

- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — ceremony repair target
- `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` section "Command Dependency Discipline" — allowlist source of truth (POSIX floor, eviction table, declared-dep principle)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`, `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fast-fixture shape + suite/manifest registration
- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — `rbq_qualify_fast`/`rbq_qualify_release`; shellcheck sibling; Step 2 deletion rationale
- `Tools/vok/vof/` — existing kit-parcel veiling (`vofr_collect` excludes vov_veiled); the linter must NOT couple to it
- Commits b7c1dbdd (retirement), 5dc8dcc6 (README restructure), b63dc6cf (foundry colophon rbw-h* -> rbw-f*)

content.text:
## Mounting ₣BB → ₢BBABM (release-ceremony-and-bcg-command-lint)

**Pace goal:** Repair the `rbk-prep-release` ceremony for the README/foundry retirements, and replace its hand-rolled bash command-audit (Step 2) with a real Rust static-analysis fixture in theurge — the **cupel** BCG command-dependency linter — that rides `rbw-ts.fast` and the release gate.

### Heat context
- **₣BB** rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification (racing, 58/66) — builds `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine, a marshal-zero-gated third qualification tier that catches silent first-build assumptions by construction.
- This pace is the ceremony-repair + command-lint slice. **It is well underway** — three commits already landed against ₢BBABM:
  - `5d59fdb08` — cupel fixture built (`rbtdru_cupel.rs` + `rbtdtu_cupel.rs`, 27 unit tests), registered in manifest/lib/suites, anchor word **cupel** minted (assay vessel, metallurgy family beside crucible/calibrant)
  - `24337f79a` — case…esac lexer state tracking (killed ~400 false positives)
  - `a35c5a493` — two more lexer false-positive classes fixed + ABANDONED-dir exclusion

So **Track 2 (the design-laden linter, including the open naming call → resolved as `cupel`) has substantially landed.** Unit tests are green (134 passing).

### What remains on the docket
The lexer is hardened, but the **first full corpus run including `vov_veiled/` — the in-pace discovery step** — and the mechanical tracks still appear open:

- **Track 2 tail** — actually run the cupel fixture over the whole corpus (the locked "expect first run not to land green" discovery), surface real eviction-table violations, fix them in-pace. Then delete ceremony **Step 2**.
- **Track 1** — ceremony retirement cleanup in `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` (Step 8 `README.consumer.md` drop, defensive `git rm` for `index.html`/`.nojekyll`, Step 10 survival-list prune, staging tweak).
- **Track 3** — fix phantom `rbw-tf.QualifyFast` → `rbw-tq.QualifyFast` in Step 11; verify-and-close items C & D.
- **Track 5** — retirement grep, foundry-colophon grep, fast qualify, onboarding smoke, notch.

### Proposed approach
1. First establish exact ground truth — confirm how far the cupel corpus run has actually gotten (does it currently pass `rbw-ts.fast`, or is the discovery run still pending?), so I know whether Track 2's tail is done or open.
2. Then walk the mechanical tracks (1, 3) and the verification sweep (5), each presented before executing per the verification gates.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — the remaining cupel-violation triage needs BCG judgment, and the ceremony edits are interdependent in one file. Not parallelizable.

The silks fit the docket well — no rename needed.

One thing I want to confirm before touching anything: **has the cupel fixture's first full-corpus discovery run actually happened yet?** The commits fix lexer false-positives (which implies it *was* run against the corpus), but the docket frames "surface violations before fixing" as a gate. Want me to start by running `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` to see current real state — or do you already know where that stands and want me to jump to a specific track?

message.content: Yes, and research the codebase accordingly to form an opinion

content.text: I'll run the fast suite (ground truth on the cupel fixture) and research the codebase in parallel.

**Verdict on Violations:**

- Lines 77-78: Partitions kit-bash vs GCB-bash by path prefix (`rbgj*` dirs)
- Lines 82-83: Skips ABANDONED* directories during walk (zrbtdru_walk_sh, lines 753-760)

**Encoded Allowlists:**

content.text:
Confirmed both signals. I now have a firm, evidence-backed read. Here's my opinion.

## Ground truth

Fast suite: **129 passed, 1 failed** — the single failure is `rbtdru_kit_bash`. This is precisely the docket's predicted "first full run will not land green" discovery step, not a regression. The lexer itself is solid (all 27 cupel unit tests pass; the case/arithmetic/array false-positive classes from the three prior commits are gone). What's failing is the *corpus*, not the linter.

## The 161 findings stratify into four clean buckets

**1. A genuine latent bug the linter caught** — `rbgb_Buckets.sh:355-356` calls `buc_warning`, which **does not exist**. The real function is `buc_warn()` (`buc_command.sh:89`). Both calls sit in bucket-delete *error* branches (HTTP 409 / catch-all), so they'd only fire on a real delete failure — the bug has been latent, never tripped by a green test. The two-pass function collector correctly didn't whitelist it because there's nothing to whitelist. **This is cupel earning its existence on its first real run.** It's a fix-in-pace, full stop.

**2. Real BCG violations in release kit-bash (buk + rbk)** — ~40 findings: `wc`/`tr`/`head`/`grep`/`awk`/`cut`/`base64` evictions (builtin replacements exist) plus "unknown" utilities (`cat`→`$(<f)`, `diff`/`cmp`/`tar`/`tail`/`stat`/`tee`/`uname`/`gdate` — no clean builtin). These are the locked "first-run cleanup is in-pace" work. The eviction-table ones are mechanical; the no-builtin ones (`diff`, `tar`, `tail -n`, `stat`) force a real choice: rewrite, or **declare as a dependency** (RBS0 + BCG) — and amending BCG's declared-dep set is a covenant conversation, not a unilateral edit.

**3. Out-of-release-scope kits** — ~70 findings in `apck/`, `lmci/`, `jjk/jjfp_fundus.sh`, `vok/`, `vslk/`, `vvk/`. These were **never written to BCG** and **don't ship in the recipe-bottle consumer release** (the ceremony ships buk + rbk, strips the rest). `jjfp_fundus.sh` alone is 19 findings of macOS/Linux test-host provisioning (`useradd`, `sysadminctl`, `dscl`, `chown`…). Holding these to strict BCG is a category error.

**4. Scratch / dead / diagnostic** — ~21 findings: `xxx_rbn.info.sh` (a stale "RBN SPLIT IMPLEMENTATION PLAN" note file, `xxx_` prefix), `rbo.observe.sh` (a `echo "RBO DIAG:"` diagnostic), `vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh` (FUTURE = dead, same shape as the already-excluded ABANDONED).

## My opinion

**The discovery falsified an assumption baked into locked decision #4.** That decision says "lint ALL `Tools/` bash … the only partition is kit-bash vs GCB-bash" — written as if `Tools/` were essentially buk + rbk release bash. It isn't: `Tools/` holds ~a dozen independent kits, most not part of the recipe-bottle release. So ~90 of the 161 findings (buckets 3 + 4) are noise against kits and scratch files that the linter has no business gating.

The covenant says when a locked line reads as false against new evidence, I raise it rather than route around it — so I'm flagging it rather than just narrowing the walk myself.

**What I'd do:** narrow the cupel corpus to the release-relevant kit-bash — `Tools/buk/` + `Tools/rbk/` (non-GCB), the exact scope the old Step 2 audited — and exclude the non-shipping kits and scratch/dead files the same way `ABANDONED*` is already excluded. Then fix bucket 1 (the `buc_warn` bug) and bucket 2 (the genuine buk/rbk violations) in-pace, surfacing the no-builtin-replacement cases to you as a small BCG declared-dep conversation before I touch them. That preserves cupel's whole value — guarding what actually ships — without conscripting a dozen unrelated kits into a discipline they never signed up for.

The alternative reading — that "ALL `Tools/`" is load-bearing and you *want* apck/lmci/vok/etc. brought to BCG too — is a much larger commitment (it makes this pace a multi-kit cleanup) and I don't think it's what the release ceremony's Step 2 replacement was scoped to do. But it's your call, because it touches a locked decision.

**The decision I need from you:** scope. Three shapes —

- **(A, my recommendation)** narrow to buk + rbk release kit-bash; exclude non-shipping kits + scratch/dead; fix buckets 1–2 in-pace.
- **(B)** keep "all `Tools/`" but exclude only obvious scratch/dead (`xxx_*`, `rbo.observe`, `FUTURE/`); treat the non-release kits as real work (large).
- **(C)** something in between — e.g., per-kit opt-in, so kits adopt BCG-strict deliberately rather than by default.

Which way do you want to take the scope — and do you want me to walk you through the buk/rbk bucket-2 findings (the declare-vs-rewrite calls) regardless, since those are real either way?

lastPrompt: Yes, and research the codebase accordingly to form an opinion

message.content: A is a good choice honestly.

=== rbo.observe.sh: full content ===
#!/bin/bash

echo "RBO DIAG: Script starting, pwd=$(pwd)"
echo "RBO DIAG: Script path=$0"
echo "RBO DIAG: Environment check..."

echo "RBO: Beginning network observation script"

set -e

# Validate required environment variables
: "${RBM_MACHINE:?}"              && echo "RBO: RBM_MACHINE              = ${RBM_MACHINE}"
: "${RBM_MONIKER:?}"              && echo "RBO: RBM_MONIKER              = ${RBM_MONIKER}"
: "${RBM_ENCLAVE_NETWORK:?}"      && echo "RBO: RBM_ENCLAVE_NETWORK      = ${RBM_ENCLAVE_NETWORK}"
: "${RBM_SENTRY_CONTAINER:?}"     && echo "RBO: RBM_SENTRY_CONTAINER     = ${RBM_SENTRY_CONTAINER}"
: "${RBM_BOTTLE_CONTAINER:?}"     && echo "RBO: RBM_BOTTLE_CONTAINER     = ${RBM_BOTTLE_CONTAINER}"
: "${RBM_PENTACLE_CONTAINER:?}"     && echo "RBO: RBM_PENTACLE_CONTAINER     = ${RBM_PENTACLE_CONTAINER}"

echo "RBO: Storing terminal control sequences"
BOLD=$(tput bold)
RED=$(tput setaf 1)
YELLOW=$(tput setaf 3)
BLUE=$(tput setaf 4)
MAGENTA=$(tput setaf 5)
WHITE=$(tput setaf 7)
RESET=$(tput sgr0)

echo "RBO: Setting up signal handling"
cleanup() {
    echo "RBO: Cleaning up observation processes"
    kill 0
    exit 0
}
trap cleanup INT TERM

echo "RBO DIAG: About to setup tcpdump"

echo "RBO: Discovering bridge interface for enclave network"
BRIDGE_INTERFACE=$(podman --connection "${RBM_MACHINE}" network inspect "${RBM_ENCLAVE_NETWORK}" --format '{{.NetworkInterface}}')
echo "RBO: Bridge interface: ${BRIDGE_INTERFACE}"

echo "RBO: Defining output prefixing functions"
prefix_bottle() {
    while read -r line; do
        echo "${YELLOW}${BOLD}RBO: [BOTTLE/PENTACLE]${RESET} $line"
    done
}

prefix_bridge() {
    while read -r line; do
        echo "${BLUE}${BOLD}RBO: [BRIDGE]${RESET} $line"
    done
}

prefix_sentry() {
    while read -r line; do
        echo "${WHITE}${BOLD}RBO: [SENTRY]${RESET} $line"
    done
}

echo "RBO: Starting network capture processes"

echo "RBO: Starting bottle/pentacle shared namespace capture (using PENTACLE container)"
podman --connection "${RBM_MACHINE}" exec "${RBM_PENTACLE_CONTAINER}" tcpdump -U -l -nn -vvv -i eth0 2>&1 |
    prefix_bottle &

echo "RBO: Starting bridge perspective capture"
podman --connection "${RBM_MACHINE}" machine ssh "sudo -n tcpdump -U -l -nn -vvv -i ${BRIDGE_INTERFACE}" 2>&1 |
    prefix_bridge &

echo "RBO: Starting sentry enclave interface capture"
podman --connection "${RBM_MACHINE}" exec "${RBM_SENTRY_CONTAINER}" tcpdump -U -l -nn -vvv -i eth1 2>&1 |
    prefix_sentry &

echo "RBO: All capture processes started"
echo "RBO: Network topology:"
echo "RBO:   - SENTRY: Bridge (eth0) <-> Internet, Enclave (eth1) <-> Internal"
echo "RBO:   - BOTTLE/PENTACLE: Shared namespace on Enclave network (eth0)"
echo "RBO: Press Ctrl+C to stop captures"

echo "RBO DIAG: About to wait for processes"

wait

echo "RBO DIAG: Wait completed"

# Cleanup will be handled by the trap

content.text:
Both are corpus-narrowing artifacts that expose real structure — and they tell me my first cut was slightly too blunt:

- **`zjjz_kindle`** (`rbhw0_cli.sh:46`) — **legitimate cross-kit dependency**: rbk's Windows handbook explicitly `source`s `jjk/jjz_zipper.sh` and calls its kindle. Real, not a bug. My narrowing broke *function visibility* for it.
- **`zrbv_kindle`** (`rbv_cli.sh:67`) — defined only in the `FUTURE/` module I just excluded. So `rbv_cli.sh` is the live-tree CLI for the *parked* PodmanVM feature — present-on-disk and sourceable, just not-yet-wired.

The lesson: I conflated two things the locked two-pass design kept separate. **Pass 1 (function collection) is meant to span the whole corpus** ("collect every locally-defined function across the corpus") so cross-kit/sourced names resolve; **Pass 2 (linting) is what scope A narrows.** My first cut narrowed both. The fix restores the locked intent: collect functions corpus-wide, lint kit-roots-only. ABANDONED (truly dead, not sourceable) stays invisible to both; FUTURE (parked but present) is visible to collection, excluded from linting.

Let me correct the implementation.

content.text:
Grounding done — the samples sharpen the picture and surfaced one important nuance. Here's where it stands and the decisions I need.

## Status
Scope A landed correctly (two commits on ₢BBABM): **161 → 62** findings, both kindle false-positives resolved by the pass-split, 134 unit tests green, clean under `deny(warnings)`.

## The 62 findings, by disposition

| Disposition | Commands (count) | My call |
|---|---|---|
| **Real bug** | `buc_warning` ×2 (`rbgb_Buckets.sh:355-356`) | Fix → `buc_warn`. Definite. |
| **Eviction table — must rewrite** (locked: enforced) | `wc` ×9, `tr` ×9, `grep` ×7, `head` ×2, `awk` ×2 (29) | Rewrite to builtins. Non-negotiable per lock. |
| **Rewritable "unknown"** (clean builtin exists) | `cat` ×2 → `$(<f)`; `diff` ×5 / `cmp` ×1 → `[[ "$(<a)" == "$(<b)" ]]` (these are text-file staleness checks — string-compare works) | Rewrite. |
| **Declare-as-dependency** (no clean builtin) | `tail` ×7, `stat` ×2, `tar` ×1, `tee` ×1, `uname` ×1 (12) | RBS0 + BCG amendment. |
| **Dev tooling** | `cargo` ×3 (`rbte_engine.sh` — theurge build) | Declare as developer dep, beside `shellcheck`. |
| **Dead code** | `tput` ×7 (`rbo.observe.sh`) | Delete the script (−7). |
| **Guarded-optional** | `gdate` ×1 (`bud_dispatch.sh:256`) | See below — it's already BCG-honorable. |

## The `gdate` insight (linter-quality signal)
`bud_dispatch.sh:256` is **already correct Pale-conduct code**:
```bash
if command -v gdate >/dev/null 2>&1; then gdate +'…%N' …; else date +'…000000000' …; fi
```
`gdate` is a *probed optional* with a `date` fallback (nanosecond precision on macOS) — exactly how BCG says to portably reach a non-floor command. Cupel flags it because the lexer doesn't know it sits behind a `command -v` guard. This is a **false positive in spirit**, and there may be others among the 62. Two ways to handle: (a) teach cupel to skip a command that's guarded by a `command -v <cmd>` probe in the same function (a real lexer refinement), or (b) special-case `gdate`. I lean (a) — it's the principled fix and likely clears other guarded optionals too.

## Three decisions for you

1. **Ratify the declare set** — `tail`, `stat`, `tar`, `tee`, `uname`, `cargo` (and possibly `cmp` if you'd rather declare than rewrite). Declaring means amending **BCG's declared-dependency list + the RBS0 Dependency Inventory + cupel's `ZRBTDRU_DECLARED_DEPS`** — and per the covenant, BCG changes happen with you, not unilaterally. Does that split look right, or do you want any of those rewritten instead?

2. **`rbo.observe.sh`** — confirm I can delete it (dead: retired `RBM_*` namespace, zero refs, superseded by `rbw-cs.Scry`).

3. **The `gdate`/guarded-optional handling** — pursue the cupel `command -v` refinement (a), or just special-case it (b)?

And one framing note: this is ~37 rewrites + ~12 declarations + 1 deletion + 1 bug. The docket locked it as in-pace ("expect first run not to land green") — I'm not reopening that, but the BUK-core rewrites (`bujb_jurisdiction` grep/awk, the foundry `wc`s) touch live tested paths and I'll want to lean on the suite as I go. Happy to proceed in-pace; just flagging the real size so you're not surprised.

What are your calls?

message.content:
Do we really need special case gdate?  As a rule I want this system to have minimal, ancient dependencies only, and I don't immediate perceive gdate as a good choice over date.

Do we actually use tail, stat, tar, tee, uname?  I guess I'm surprised by that.  For good reasons?

This is a much better question than my lazy "declare them all." Looking at the actual sites, my earlier triage was too quick — several of these are *not* good reasons. Let me give you the honest per-command answer.

Let me nail down the one I'm least sure about (what `stat` is actually checking) before I present:

=== stat helper: function header + what it returns ===
      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK}"
      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK}"
      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK}"
      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "zbujb_check_key_file: invalid slot (expected priv|work): '${z_slot}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # BSD stat first (Mac), GNU stat fallback (Linux). The failed flavor's
  # stderr is overwritten by the successful flavor's invocation; on total
  # failure z_stat_stderr holds the GNU-attempt error.
  if stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
    : # macOS / BSD stat succeeded
  elif stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
    : # GNU/Linux stat succeeded
  else
    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path} — see ${z_stat_stderr}"
  fi

  local z_mode
  z_mode=$(<"${z_stat_stdout}")
  z_mode="${z_mode//$'\n'/}"
  test "${z_mode}" = "600" \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"

      > "${z_dry_stdout}" \
      2> "${z_dry_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path} — see ${z_dry_stderr}"
}
